items.title: Trump calls Artemis II astronauts "modern-day pioneers" in live conversation

items.title: Artemis II crew completes record-breaking trip around the moon
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew flew farther from Earth than any humans in history as they passed behind the far side of the moon on Monday night.
items.content: The Artemis II crew flew farther from Earth than any humans in history as they passed behind the far side of the moon on Monday night.

items.title: Watch: Trump calls Artemis II crew, congratulates them on historic trip around moon

items.title: Artemis II crew views total solar eclipse
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew observed a total solar eclipse on Monday night while making its way back to Earth. Former NASA astronaut Terry Hart joins CBS News with his reaction.
items.content: The Artemis II crew observed a total solar eclipse on Monday night while making its way back to Earth. Former NASA astronaut Terry Hart joins CBS News with his reaction.

items.title: Retired astronaut Cady Coleman says Artemis II mission "showed us just what can be done by people"

items.content: The Artemis II crew is now on its way back to Earth after flying behind the moon. Retired NASA astronaut Cady Coleman joins CBS News with her thoughts.

items.title: How Artemis II crew will spend last days of mission
items.standfirst: The four history-making Artemis II astronauts are now on their way back to Earth. Retired NASA astronaut Bonnie Dunbar joins CBS News to explain what the crew will be doing for the final few days of its mission before splashdown on Friday.
items.content: The four history-making Artemis II astronauts are now on their way back to Earth. Retired NASA astronaut Bonnie Dunbar joins CBS News to explain what the crew will be doing for the final few days of its mission before splashdown on Friday.

items.title: Watch: Artemis II mission loses contact with Earth for 40 minutes

items.content: Once contact was regained, astronaut Christina Koch said: "It is so great to hear from Earth again."

items.title: Artemis II travels around far side of the moon
items.standfirst: Artemis II on Monday broke the Apollo 13 mission's record for farthest distance humans have travelled from Earth as the crew looped around the far side of the moon. Mark Strassmann reports.
items.content: Artemis II on Monday broke the Apollo 13 mission's record for farthest distance humans have travelled from Earth as the crew looped around the far side of the moon. Mark Strassmann reports.

items.title: Artemis II's Christina Koch says "it is so great to hear from Earth again" after planned blackout
items.standfirst: The crew of NASA's Artemis II mission has made its way around the far side of the moon, witnessing parts of the moon that have never been seen before with the naked eye. Former NASA astronaut Terry Hart joins with his reaction. Then, CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood joins with analysis.
items.content: The crew of NASA's Artemis II mission has made its way around the far side of the moon, witnessing parts of the moon that have never been seen before with the naked eye. Former NASA astronaut Terry Hart joins with his reaction. Then, CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood joins with analysis.

items.content: President Trump warns Iran is running out of time; Artemis II travels around the far side of the moon.

items.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
items.standfirst: Mission's four astronauts break record set in 1970 by Apollo 13, which flew 400,171km from the planet.
items.content: Mission's four astronauts break record set in 1970 by Apollo 13, which flew 400,171km from the planet.

items.title: NASA Crew Heads Back to Earth After Record Trip Around Moon
items.standfirst: NASA’s four Artemis astronauts swung behind the moon and are headed home, in a journey that shattered space travel distance records and brought people the closest they’ve been to the lunar surface in more than 50 years.
items.content: NASA’s four Artemis astronauts swung behind the moon and are headed home, in a journey that shattered space travel distance records and brought people the closest they’ve been to the lunar surface in more than 50 years.

items.content: As the astronauts pass behind the Moon they will experience a moment of silence and solitude as communication with the Earth is blocked.

items.title: Hungary pipeline false-flag claims swirl as JD Vance makes election intervention
items.standfirst: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most pollsHungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, the prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance.Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day after Serbia said it had found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Artemis II swings back around after completing record-setting moon flyby
clusters.neutral_headline: Artemis II swings back around after completing record-setting moon flyby

items.title: Artemis II swings back around after completing record-setting moon flyby
items.standfirst: Astronauts become Earth’s farthest travelled and exceed a 1970 record on the fifth day of the mission Blackouts, broken records and a message from the past: five key moments from Artemis II’s lunar flybySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxArtemis II astronauts broke Apollo 13’s distance record at 1.57pm eastern time on Monday, hugging each other in the cramped capsule as they made history for becoming the first four humans to travel the farthest from Earth.About five hours later, at 7.02pm ET, the crew reached the furthest point in its mission, before swinging back around, at 252,756 miles from Earth – 4,111 miles farther than the ill-fated Apollo 13 mission in 1970. Continue reading...
items.content: Astronauts become Earth’s farthest travelled and exceed a 1970 record on the fifth day of the mission Blackouts, broken records and a message from the past: five key moments from Artemis II’s lunar flybySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxArtemis II astronauts broke Apollo 13’s distance record at 1.57pm eastern time on Monday, hugging each other in the cramped capsule as they made history for becoming the first four humans to travel the farthest from Earth.About five hours later, at 7.02pm ET, the crew reached the furthest point in its mission, before swinging back around, at 252,756 miles from Earth – 4,111 miles farther than the ill-fated Apollo 13 mission in 1970. Continue reading...

items.title: Artemis II astronauts capture rare lunar craters, solar eclipse after historic Moon flyby
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts sent detailed observations of the Moon after traveling farther from Earth than any human before, breaking Apollo 13’s distance record. Mission control regained contact after a 40-minute blackout behind the Moon, with the crew expressing relief as communications were restored with Earth.
items.content: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts sent detailed observations of the Moon after traveling farther from Earth than any human before, breaking Apollo 13’s distance record. Mission control regained contact after a 40-minute blackout behind the Moon, with the crew expressing relief as communications were restored with Earth.

clusters.title: What should patients do during England doctors’ strike? #health
clusters.neutral_headline: What should patients do during England doctors’ strike? #health
bullet_summary: The guidance for day to day planning is straightforward: seek urgent help when it’s necessary and do not put off confirmed medical appointments
The NHS is advising patients in England to only use emergency services when necessary but attend any confirmed appointments

items.title: What should patients do during England doctors’ strike? #health
items.standfirst: NHS advice during the six day strike As resident doctors in England begin a six day strike, the NHS is urging patients not to delay care they need. The guidance for day to day planning is straightforward: seek urgent help when it’s necessary and do not put off confirmed medical appointments.…

items.title: NHS strike: should patients delay care? #health
items.standfirst: NHS tells patients not to postpone care during doctors’ strikes Ahead of a six day doctors’ strike in England , the NHS has urged patients not to delay the care they need . clusters.title: Steve Hilton discusses campaign for California governor after securing Trump endorsement
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump endorses ex-UK political aide Steve Hilton for California governor
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Steve Hilton discusses campaign for California governor after securing Trump endorsement
items.standfirst: Overnight, President Trump posted on social media who he wants to succeed Gavin Newsom as the next governor of California: former Fox News host Steve Hilton, who is one of the two Republicans still in the race. Hilton joins "The Takeout" to discuss his campaign.
items.content: Overnight, President Trump posted on social media who he wants to succeed Gavin Newsom as the next governor of California: former Fox News host Steve Hilton, who is one of the two Republicans still in the race. Hilton joins "The Takeout" to discuss his campaign.

items.title: Trump endorses ex-UK political aide Steve Hilton for California governor
items.standfirst: Steve Hilton, who advised the former prime minster and hosted a Fox News show, is running as a Republican.
items.content: Steve Hilton, who advised the former prime minster and hosted a Fox News show, is running as a Republican.
